item.title: Supreme Court takes up dispute over Arizona voting restrictions
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed to decide whether Arizona can impose voting rules, including a measure that requires documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote on a state form.

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Alan Dershowitz's defamation case against CNN
item.description: The Supreme Court declined to take up former Harvard law professor Alan Dershowitz's case alleging CNN defamed him.

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear Trump's appeal of E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse case
item.description: A unanimous federal jury found that a preponderance of evidence supported Carroll's claim that Mr. Trump sexually abused her.

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks to resume after exchange of strikes, Trump says
item.description: President Trump says U.S.-Iran talks will resume, at Tehran's request, after several days of tit-for-tat strikes tested a shaky ceasefire.

item.title: Princess Kate hikes 3 mountains to raise money, awareness for cancer
item.description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, climbed three of the highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales to raise money and awareness for a cancer charity. The future queen is also revealing how cancer has changed her life. Holly Williams reports.

item.title: A look at Serena Williams' highly-anticipated return to Wimbledon
item.description: Wimbledon kicks off Monday in London and all eyes will be on the return of Serena Williams. Sports journalist and 60 Minutes correspondent Jon Wertheim previews Williams' odds and other big storylines.

item.title: Matchups to watch in the World Cup knockout stage
item.description: CBS Sports soccer analyst Ian Joy breaks down the upcoming matches in the World Cup knockout round, including if he thinks the U.S. can advance past Bosnia.

item.title: Police say 5 killed, 2 arrested after shooting at German youth center
item.description: German police say 2 people are in custody and 5 were killed in a rare shooting that took place at a youth center in Stade, near Hamburg.

item.title: 12-year-old injured by bison at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: A 12-year-old was injured by a bison while visiting Yellowstone National Park last week, the National Park Service said.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh to return to court for first time since murder convictions overturned
item.description: Alex Murdaugh is expected back in court in South Carolina on Monday for the first time since the state Supreme Court overturned his convictions for killing his son and wife. Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: Major Supreme Court cases that will test Trump's power yet to be decided
item.description: As the Supreme Court nears the end of its term, major cases have yet to be decided. Some of the cases, including birthright citizenship, will put President Trump's authority to the test. Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: Where the U.S.-Iran peace deal stands after both countries trade strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran traded strikes over the weekend, threatening the peace agreement. Now, the U.S. says both sides are talking again, but Iran has not confirmed that. Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
item.description: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelans still missing after earthquakes as search for survivors continues
item.description: Tens of thousands of people remain missing after two powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela last week. There is now a global effort to find survivors, including crews from the U.S. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Huge Supreme Court decisions imminent as term comes to an end
item.description: Several high-profile Supreme Court rulings are expected as the court nears the end of its term, including a decision on President Trump's bid to end birthright citizenship. CBS News' Jan Crawford reports.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll keeps climbing, voices pierce through the rubble
item.description: The death toll keeps climbing in Venezuela after back-to-back earthquakes rocked the South American region. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more as rescue teams dig for survivors.

item.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
item.description: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

item.title: Venezuelans race against clock to rescue earthquake survivors
item.description: Venezuelans are desperately searching for survivors after the country faced catastrophic, back-to-back deadly earthquakes. Claudia Manresa, a Project HOPE regional manager, joins with more details on rescue efforts.

item.title: Bill Cassidy blasts Trump and RFK Jr. as he prepares to leave Senate
item.description: Louisiana Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy is denouncing President Trump's administration, including Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr., despite being the key vote to advance Kennedy's nomination last year. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: Afghanistan's Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 36 civilians
item.description: Pakistani security forces Sunday carried out an intelligence-based ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan​ border, followed by "calibrated strikes."

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade attacks as Tehran claims power over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to stand down after tensions reescalated this weekend in the Strait of Hormuz, a U.S. official says. CBS News' Aaron Navarro and Aaron MacLean have more.

item.title: Dangerous heat wave impacting large swathes of the U.S. this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast a large swath of the U.S. this week. The National Weather Service says temperatures will feel hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: Pablo Escobar murder victim's son is named Colombia's interior minister
item.description: The incoming minister's father, Rodrigo Lara Bonilla, was serving as justice minister in 1984 when he was gunned down in Bogota on Pablo Escobar's orders.

item.title: Britain's Princess Kate climbs 3 mountains in 24 hours for charity
item.description: The challenge was undertaken to raise awareness for a charity she has been involved with since her own cancer treatment.

item.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
item.description: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.

item.title: U.S. official says talks will resume after Iran threatens to walk away
item.description: Iran launched drone and missile attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain on Sunday after the U.S. and Iran traded attacks earlier over the weekend.

item.title: 6/28: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Bill Cassidy discusses the explosive meeting between President Trump and Senate Republicans as well as why he changed his vote on the Iran war powers resolution. Plus, Sen. Tim Kaine and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: Cassidy accuses RFK Jr. of building public health "upon a foundation of lies"
item.description: Republican Sen. Bill Cassidy said of HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., "If you build public health upon a foundation of lies, then you're going to have the absence of adequate public health."

item.title: Venezuela death toll rises as search for earthquake survivors continues
item.description: Venezuelans have taken the search for missing loved ones into their own hands, citing a scarcity of government rescuers.

item.title: Serena Williams will return to singles tennis at Wimbledon, as a wild card
item.description: Serena Williams recently returned to competition​ in doubles after nearly four years away from professional tennis.
